channel.title: AngiospermEvolution on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in AngiospermEvolution on emsenn.net

item.title: Double Fertilization
item.description: Double fertilization: the defining reproductive innovation of angiosperms, in which a single pollen tube delivers two sperm cells to the ovule. One sperm fuses with the egg cell to form the diploid zygote that will develop into the embryo; the other fuses with the central cell’s two polar nuclei to form the triploid endosperm. This two-for-one event is unique to flowering plants and ensures that endosperm is produced only when fertilization occurs, eliminating wasted provisioning of unfertilized ovules.
